curriculum.subject: Biology
curriculum.overallDescription: 10 units, 32 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: The Living World, Biological Classification, Plant Kingdom, Animal Kingdom

units.unitName: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals
units.description: Morphology of Flowering Plants, Anatomy of Flowering Plants, Structural Organisation in Animals
Cell-The Unit of Life, Biomolecules, Cell Cycle and Cell Division
Photosynthesis in Higher Plants, Respiration in Plants, Plant - Growth and Development
Breathing and Exchange of Gases, Body Fluids and Circulation, Excretory Products and their Elimination, Locomotion and Movement, Neural Control and Coordination, Chemical Coordination and Integration
Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants, Human Reproduction, Reproductive Health
topics: Flower structure and development of gametophytes
Pollination types, agencies, out-breeding devices, pollen-pistil interaction
Double fertilization and post-fertilization events (endosperm/embryo)
Development of seed and fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
Male and female reproductive systems; microscopic anatomy of testis and ovary
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, embryo development to blastocyst
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
Need for reproductive health and prevention of STDs
Birth control methods, contraception, MTP
Amniocentesis
Infertility and assisted reproductive technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)

units.description: Principles of Inheritance and Variation, Molecular Basis of Inheritance, Evolution
topics: Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance; Sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, sex-linked inheritance disorders
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
Search for genetic material; Structure of DNA and RNA
DNA packaging, replication, Central Dogma (transcription/translation)
Genetic code and gene expression regulation (lac operon)
Human genome project, rice genome project
DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanism of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination

units.description: Human Health and Diseases, Microbes in Human Welfare
topics: Digestion and absorption processes (alimentary canal, enzymes)
Nutritional disorders: PEM, indigestion, constipation, jaundice, diarrhea
Role of microbes in human welfare (implied from context)

units.description: Biotechnology - Principles and Processes, Biotechnology and its Applications
Organisms and Populations, Ecosystem, Biodiversity and its Conservation

practicals.title: Study locally available common flowering plants from family Solanaceae including dissection and display of floral whorls
practicals.details: Show number of chambers (floral formulae and floral diagrams), type of root, stem, leaf arrangement

practicals.title: Preparation and study of T.S. of dicot and monocot roots and stems
practicals.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

practicals.title: Study of osmosis by potato osmometer
practicals.details: Demonstrate endosmosis in raisins or potato

practicals.title: Study of plasmolysis in epidermal peels
practicals.details: Using Rhoeo/lily leaves or onion bulb scale leaves

practicals.title: Study of distribution of stomata on upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Comparative study of the rates of transpiration in the upper and lower surfaces of leaves
Test for presence of sugar, starch, proteins and fats in plant and animal materials
Separation of plant pigments through paper chromatography
practicals.details: Chromatographic separation of chlorophylls and carotenoids

practicals.title: Study of the rate of respiration in flower buds/leaf tissue and germinating seeds
practicals.details: Measure CO2 production or O2 consumption

practicals.title: Test for presence of urea, sugar, albumin and bile salts in urine
Study of pollen germination on stigma
practicals.details: Temporary mount observation or SEM analysis

practicals.title: Study the plant population density by quadrat method
Study the plant population frequency by quadrat method
practicals.details: Frequency analysis in a given area

practicals.title: Prepare temporary mount of onion root tip to study mitosis
Isolate DNA from available plant material such as spinach, green pea seeds, papaya, banana etc.
practicals.details: Extraction and visualization of genomic DNA

practicals.title: Study flowers adapted to pollination by different agencies (wind, insects, birds)
Identification of stages of gamete development (T.S. of testis and ovary)
Study meiosis in onion bud cell or grasshopper testis through permanent slides
Study T.S. of blastula through permanent slides (Mammalian)
Demonstrate Mendelian inheritance using seeds of different colour/sizes
Prepare pedigree charts for genetic traits (rolling tongue, blood groups, etc.)
Controlled pollination - emasculation, tagging and bagging
Identification of disease causing organisms (Ascaris, Entamoeba, Plasmodium, fungi)
Study symbiotic association in lichens, root nodules of leguminous plants, parasitic mode (Cuscuta)
Study morphological adaptations in xerophytic conditions using Cactus/Opuntia models
practicals.details: Analysis of structural features for water conservation

projects.title: Investigatory Project
projects.details: Project Record + Viva Voce (Credit to the student's work over the academic session may be given)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI & XII Theory Typology: VSA (including MCQs), Assertion-Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions.
Internal Choice: Approximately 33% internal choice provided in question papers.
competency_weightage: Class XI & XII Theory Competencies Distribution: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).
Suggestive Verbs for Knowledge/Understanding: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize.
Suggestive Verbs for Application: Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish.
bloom_levels: Level 1 (Knowledge): Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding - State concepts, list facts, define terms.
Level 2 (Application): Apply of Knowledge/Concepts - Explain phenomena, calculate values, illustrate processes.
Level 3 (Higher Order Thinking Skills): Analyse, Evaluate and Create - Interpret data, compare contrasts, evaluate arguments, construct models.
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment Topics: Digestion and Absorption (Class XI), Environmental Issues (Class XII). These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Visually Impaired Students Written Test: 30 minutes duration, containing 15 practical skill-based very short answer questions. Student required to answer any 10 questions.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Evaluation Scheme: One Major Experiment (Experiments 1, 3, 7, or 8) - 5 marks; One Minor Experiment (Experiments 6, 9-13) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 2, 4, 5) - 5 marks; Spotting Part B - 7 marks.
Class XII Practical Evaluation Scheme: Major Experiments (Nos. 2 & 3) - 5 marks; Minor Experiment (No. 1 or 4) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 1 & 4) - 5 marks; Spotting - 7 marks.

Visually Impaired Practical Exam: Time 02 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva Voce (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project: Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI & XII Theory Typology: VSA (including MCQs), Assertion-Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions.
Internal Choice: Approximately 33% internal choice provided in question papers.
competency_weightage: Class XI & XII Theory Competencies Distribution: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).
Suggestive Verbs for Knowledge/Understanding: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize.
Suggestive Verbs for Application: Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish.
bloom_levels: Level 1 (Knowledge): Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding - State concepts, list facts, define terms.
Level 2 (Application): Apply of Knowledge/Concepts - Explain phenomena, calculate values, illustrate processes.
Level 3 (Higher Order Thinking Skills): Analyse, Evaluate and Create - Interpret data, compare contrasts, evaluate arguments, construct models.
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment Topics: Digestion and Absorption (Class XI), Environmental Issues (Class XII). These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Visually Impaired Students Written Test: 30 minutes duration, containing 15 practical skill-based very short answer questions. Student required to answer any 10 questions.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Evaluation Scheme: One Major Experiment (Experiments 1, 3, 7, or 8) - 5 marks; One Minor Experiment (Experiments 6, 9-13) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 2, 4, 5) - 5 marks; Spotting Part B - 7 marks.
Class XII Practical Evaluation Scheme: Major Experiments (Nos. 2 & 3) - 5 marks; Minor Experiment (No. 1 or 4) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 1 & 4) - 5 marks; Spotting - 7 marks.

Visually Impaired Practical Exam: Time 02 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva Voce (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project: Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competency_groups.description: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes
competencies: C-3.1 Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C-3.2 Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C-3.3 Describes mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competency_groups.description: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms; C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms); C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
competencies: C-4.1 Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic/ heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five-kingdoms
C-4.2 Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C-4.3 Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competency_groups.description: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)
competencies: C-5.3 Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history and the present times to the overall field of Science, including the disciplines that constitute it
competency_groups.description: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner
competencies: C-6.1 Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge in order to appreciate that Science is ever evolving, and that there are still many unanswered questions
competency_groups.description: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students; C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate
competencies: C-7.1 States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied — ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C-7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised to be inadequate

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competency_groups.description: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results; C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)
competencies: C-8.1 Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C-8.2 Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws, and principles, communicates findings using scientific terminology)

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit I (Diversity of Living Organisms): 15 marks; Unit II (Structural Organization in Plants and Animals): 10 marks; Unit III (Cell: Structure and Function): 15 marks; Unit IV (Plant Physiology): 12 marks; Unit V (Human Physiology): 18 marks.

Class XI Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting Part B: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.

Class XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 Hours. Unit VI (Reproduction): 16 marks; Unit VII (Genetics and Evolution): 20 marks; Unit VIII (Biology and Human Welfare): 12 marks; Unit IX (Biotechnology and its Applications): 12 marks; Unit X (Ecology and Environment): 10 marks.

Class XII Practicals: Max Marks 30, Time 3 Hours. One Major Experiment: 5 marks; One Minor Experiment: 4 marks; Slide Preparation: 5 marks; Spotting: 7 marks; Practical Record + Viva Voce: 4 marks; Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce: 5 marks.
question_paper_design: Class XI & XII Theory Typology: VSA (including MCQs), Assertion-Reasoning type questions, SA, LA-I, LA-II, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions.
Internal Choice: Approximately 33% internal choice provided in question papers.
competency_weightage: Class XI & XII Theory Competencies Distribution: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (50%), Application of Knowledge / Concepts (30%), Analyse, Evaluate and Create (20%).
Suggestive Verbs for Knowledge/Understanding: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize.
Suggestive Verbs for Application: Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish.
bloom_levels: Level 1 (Knowledge): Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding - State concepts, list facts, define terms.
Level 2 (Application): Apply of Knowledge/Concepts - Explain phenomena, calculate values, illustrate processes.
Level 3 (Higher Order Thinking Skills): Analyse, Evaluate and Create - Interpret data, compare contrasts, evaluate arguments, construct models.
internal_assessment: Formative Assessment Topics: Digestion and Absorption (Class XI), Environmental Issues (Class XII). These topics are included in the syllabus but assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Visually Impaired Students Written Test: 30 minutes duration, containing 15 practical skill-based very short answer questions. Student required to answer any 10 questions.
practical_assessment: Class XI Practical Evaluation Scheme: One Major Experiment (Experiments 1, 3, 7, or 8) - 5 marks; One Minor Experiment (Experiments 6, 9-13) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 2, 4, 5) - 5 marks; Spotting Part B - 7 marks.
Class XII Practical Evaluation Scheme: Major Experiments (Nos. 2 & 3) - 5 marks; Minor Experiment (No. 1 or 4) - 4 marks; Slide Preparation (Experiments 1 & 4) - 5 marks; Spotting - 7 marks.

Visually Impaired Practical Exam: Time 02 Hours, Max Marks 30. Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (5 marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 marks), Practical Records (5 marks), Viva Voce (10 marks).
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
Class XII Investigatory Project: Investigatory Project and its Project Record + Viva Voce carries 5 marks.
